symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional retrosternal chest tightness relieved by rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional chest tightness relieved by rest is the defining symptom of angina.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radiation to the left arm and jaw

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Arm/jaw radiation is a classic feature of cardiac ischaemic pain.
A crescendo pattern defines unstable angina and imminent infarction risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Associated nausea and mild exertional dyspnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic accompaniments support an ischaemic cardiac cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active smoking and irregular statin adherence (high cardiovascular risk)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Smoking and untreated hyperlipidaemia drive coronary atherosclerosis and raise pre-test probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain not reproducible on palpation and not related to meals/posture

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-reproducible, non-positional pain argues against a musculoskeletal or reflux cause.
An S4 reflects reduced LV compliance from ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ischaemic ECG changes/troponin dynamics and reversible perfusion defects on stress testing

investigation_details.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG, serial troponin, and stress echo/angiography
investigation_details.expected_result: Possible ischaemic ECG changes with/without troponin rise; reversible perfusion defects or a flow-limiting coronary stenosis on functional/anatomical testing
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Unstable angina/ACS cannot be diagnosed or risk-stratified and an evolving infarction may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective ischaemia on ECG/troponin/stress testing confirms coronary ischaemic disease.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Exertional arm/jaw-radiating chest tightness relieved by rest, new and accelerating over 48 hours, in a smoker with hyperlipidaemia, indicates unstable angina requiring urgent same-day cardiac assessment and interim aspirin/GTN.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for ischaemic changes; must be done urgently even though it can be normal between episodes.
Detects myocardial injury and risk-stratifies ACS.
Assesses inducible ischaemia and coronary anatomy once acute ACS is excluded/stabilised.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient referral without same-day ECG/troponin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deferring assessment of new accelerating angina risks progression to myocardial infarction.
